last_item: Roles & Databases — scope the single `dropBusy` boolean per-row so unrelated Delete buttons stay enabled during a drop (high/S).
last_result: REJECTED (self, on restraint — decisive evidence, no review panel). The premise is false: `dropBusy` is true iff `doDrop` runs, and `doDrop` is only reachable from the modal `TypedConfirmDialog` (open iff dropTarget !== null). So during the entire busy window a modal AlertDialog is up and Radix marks the background table inert/aria-hidden — proven by a written test where an unrelated row's Delete button is in the DOM but unreachable via getByRole. On success the dialog closes AND reloadAll() swaps the tables for a Spinner before busy clears. No user can ever see or click an 'unrelated frozen row,' so per-row scoping adds conditional logic for zero observable payoff. Reverted code + test; recorded the lesson (a global busy flag that only flips under a modal is already effectively scoped — the modal gates it) in learnings.md; marked backlog item rejected. No code shipped. Next top item: Dashboard — the 'no backup yet' callout says 'Run one from the Backups page' as plain text; make 'Backups page' a <Link to="/backups"> (med/S).
notes: PURE UX-IMPROVEMENT LOOP. Sibling of the COMPLETED scripts/ralph/ shadcn-migration track. Capability parity is sacred (Vitest suite is the contract); layout/flow/copy are free to change. Restraint over churn — rejecting a change and recording why is a normal outcome. Frontend/UX only, never touch Go. go build is blocked in-sandbox (snap-confine); run with sandbox disabled. See PROMPT.md + UX-PRINCIPLES.md + PERSONAS.md + UI-RULES.md.
north_star: The indiepg panel is the easiest, clearest, safest way for an indie hacker to run their Postgres — minimal surface, co-located workflows, honest state, no over-design. Stop when the UX has converged (stable_streak >= 3).
